 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4mjap","title":"[REVIEW][LOW] fcp-test-connector needs handshake counter probe for j1pjg coalesce regression","description":"crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-test-connector.rs handle_handshake currently mints a fresh SessionId on every call but exposes no counter. The j1pjg regression test subprocess_connector_concurrent_same_zone_invokes_all_succeed asserts no-deadlock + all-succeed, but cannot assert the coalescer lands exactly one handshake. Suggested fix: add AtomicUsize HANDSHAKE_COUNT incremented in handle_handshake and exposed via a new JSON-RPC method or via self_check metadata, then strengthen the regression test to assert count == 1 after the 8 concurrent invokes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:09:20.423571Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:56:41.276831Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:56:41.275953Z","close_reason":"Added a handshake counter probe to fcp-test-connector and surfaced it through self_check details for j1pjg regression assertions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4mk","title":"fcp.reddit: Subreddit Browsing and Search","description":"# fcp.reddit: Subreddit Browsing and Search (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable discovery of relevant communities and threads:
- browse subreddits
- search for subreddits
- search posts across subreddit/all

## Scope
- Get subreddit metadata (rules, description, NSFW/quarantine flags).
- List posts by sort (hot/new/top).
- Search posts with query + filters.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Reddit API hosts.
- Content is untrusted/tainted by default.
- Respect content restrictions:
  - NSFW/quarantined handling must be policy-controlled

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.subreddit.get`
  - `reddit.subreddit.search`
  - `reddit.subreddit.posts.list`
  - `reddit.search.posts`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - rate limit headers handling
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Browsing/search is reliable and policy-respecting.

Reddit now has full subreddit browsing. 15 ops total, all tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:51:13Z"},{"id":1363,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-4mk","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented subreddit.rs: SubredditInfo, PostSort/TimeFilter, SearchQuery builder, ContentFilter with NSFW/quarantine blocking. 139 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:57:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4n0q","title":"TEST: fcp-streaming Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-streaming Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for streaming and SSE library.

## Test Categories

### Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- [ ] Event parsing (data, event, id, retry fields)
- [ ] Multi-line data handling
- [ ] Event ID tracking for reconnection
- [ ] Retry interval handling
- [ ] Connection keep-alive

### WebSocket Support
- [ ] Connection establishment
- [ ] Message framing (text, binary)
- [ ] Ping/pong handling
- [ ] Graceful close
- [ ] Reconnection with backoff

### Stream Processing
- [ ] Backpressure handling
- [ ] Buffer management
- [ ] Stream cancellation
- [ ] Timeout enforcement

### Error Recovery
- [ ] Network disconnection
- [ ] Partial message handling
- [ ] Reconnection with last-event-id
- [ ] Maximum reconnection attempts

### Performance
- [ ] Zero-copy parsing where possible
- [ ] Memory allocation limits
- [ ] Large message handling

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock SSE endpoints returning controlled event streams
- Mock WebSocket servers with programmable responses
- Inject network errors at specific points

## Coverage Target: >90%","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:12:35.391386702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:44.164262726Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:44.164262726Z","close_reason":"Streaming functionality is now part of FCP2 SDK (1n78.20.1). The SDK Streaming bead already covers EventEnvelope, replay, and acks.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-4n0q","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9co","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-4p4ti","title":"TEST-FUZZ-1: fuzz fcp-protocol SymbolRequest CBOR decode + validate + verify path","description":"The control-plane SymbolRequest is a critical mesh-side parser/validator. Today fuzz/ has targets for FCPS frames, OAuth, raptorq, websocket, SSE, and host invoke dispatch — but nothing exercises the SymbolRequest CBOR decode path or its validate_hint_bounds / validate_bounds / verify validators. Add fuzz_symbol_request_validate target that decodes arbitrary bytes as SymbolRequest via ciborium and, if decoding succeeds, exercises validate_hint_bounds, validate_bounds(true|false), has_proof_of_need, transcript_bytes, and verify with a fixed Ed25519 key. Property: no panic on any input. Anti-amplification cap MAX_SYMBOLS_HARD_CAP=2001 and MAX_MISSING_HINT_ENTRIES=100 should never be bypassed by malformed inputs. Belongs in cc_2 domain (fcp-protocol/fcps.rs and fcp-mesh symbol_request handler are the receivers).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:35:47.713698Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:42:02.318967Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","mesh","protocol","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2976,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-4p4ti","author":"jemanuel","text":"4274363e lands fuzz_targets/symbol_request_validate.rs (Cargo.toml binary entry was bundled into the prior session-metamorphic commit ad125bf0). Caps input at 64KiB, decodes via ciborium, then drives validate_hint_bounds, validate_bounds(true|false), has_proof_of_need, transcript_bytes, and verify with a deterministic Ed25519 key. Only oracle is panic-freedom. Verified clean via 'cargo +nightly fuzz check fuzz_symbol_request_validate' (release profile, ~1m 36s).","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:42:02Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-4p4ti","title":"TEST-FUZZ-1: fuzz fcp-protocol SymbolRequest CBOR decode + validate + verify path","description":"The control-plane SymbolRequest is a critical mesh-side parser/validator. Today fuzz/ has targets for FCPS frames, OAuth, raptorq, websocket, SSE, and host invoke dispatch — but nothing exercises the SymbolRequest CBOR decode path or its validate_hint_bounds / validate_bounds / verify validators. Add fuzz_symbol_request_validate target that decodes arbitrary bytes as SymbolRequest via ciborium and, if decoding succeeds, exercises validate_hint_bounds, validate_bounds(true|false), has_proof_of_need, transcript_bytes, and verify with a fixed Ed25519 key. Property: no panic on any input. Anti-amplification cap MAX_SYMBOLS_HARD_CAP=2001 and MAX_MISSING_HINT_ENTRIES=100 should never be bypassed by malformed inputs. Belongs in cc_2 domain (fcp-protocol/fcps.rs and fcp-mesh symbol_request handler are the receivers).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:35:47.713698Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:42:02.745486Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:42:02.744903Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","mesh","protocol","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2976,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-4p4ti","author":"jemanuel","text":"4274363e lands fuzz_targets/symbol_request_validate.rs (Cargo.toml binary entry was bundled into the prior session-metamorphic commit ad125bf0). Caps input at 64KiB, decodes via ciborium, then drives validate_hint_bounds, validate_bounds(true|false), has_proof_of_need, transcript_bytes, and verify with a deterministic Ed25519 key. Only oracle is panic-freedom. Verified clean via 'cargo +nightly fuzz check fuzz_symbol_request_validate' (release profile, ~1m 36s).","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:42:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4px2f","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-webhook/handler.rs: expired replay-cache entries can pin ReplayCacheFull for up to 60s","description":"file: crates/fcp-webhook/src/handler.rs:297-325

repro:
1. Build a WebhookHandler with idempotency enabled and idempotency_ttl set to a short duration such as 1ms.
2. Fill the replay cache to MAX_SEEN_EVENTS with unique IDs.
3. Wait for the TTL to expire, but for less than 60 seconds.
4. Call claim_event() with a fresh event_id.
5. claim_event returns ReplayCacheFull because cleanup_seen_events() skips pruning until CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MILLIS elapses, even though the cache entries are already expired.

suggested fix:
On the cache-full path, force a stale-entry prune before returning ReplayCacheFull, or tie the cleanup cadence to min(idempotency_ttl, CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MILLIS) so expired entries cannot block new deliveries for up to a minute.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:07.593351Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:26:26.514804Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:26:26.514259Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2899,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-4px2f","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verified work landed in commit 0b788d26 (2026-04-25): added prune_expired_events_locked() helper called on the cache-full path in both claim_event() and record_event() before returning ReplayCacheFull, plus regression test test_claim_event_prunes_expired_entries_when_cache_is_full exercising MAX_SEEN_EVENTS expired entries with skipped periodic cleanup. All 86 fcp-webhook handler tests pass. Closing bead.","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:26:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4st","title":"fcp.spotify: Playlist CRUD Operations","description":"# fcp.spotify: Playlist CRUD Operations (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe playlist workflows:
- list playlists
- create/update playlists
- add/remove/reorder tracks

Playlists are user-facing state; writes must be auditable.

## Scope
- List user playlists (pagination).
- Create playlist.
- Update playlist metadata (name/description/public/private).
- Add/remove/reorder tracks.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.playlists.read`
- `spotify.playlists.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include playlist id + action + counts
  - never log track names or user data

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.playlist.list`
  - `spotify.playlist.get`
  - `spotify.playlist.create`
  - `spotify.playlist.update`
  - `spotify.playlist.tracks.add`
  - `spotify.playlist.tracks.remove`
  - `spotify.playlist.tracks.reorder`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - add/remove/reorder correctness
  - audit/receipt for writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Playlist changes are reliable, idempotent where possible, and auditable.

All with capability gating (playlists.read/playlists.write). Spotify connector now at 33 operations. 283 tests pass, golden vectors verified.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:30:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4t7t","title":"fcp.bigquery: Google BigQuery data warehouse connector","description":"## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant BigQuery connector for querying and dataset/table management with strict NetworkConstraints and audit for writes.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `storage`
- `operational`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response (SQL) + polling for long queries

## Overview
Google BigQuery data warehouse connector for the Flywheel Connector Platform. Provides comprehensive access to BigQuery's analytics and data warehouse capabilities.

## Value Assessment
- **Value Score**: 49/100 (Tier 4)
- **Archetype**: Request-Response
- **Primary Use Cases**: Analytics, data warehousing, business intelligence, large-scale data processing

## Operations

### Query Zone (read-only analytics)
- **query.execute**: Execute SQL queries with parameterized inputs
- **query.dry_run**: Validate queries and estimate costs without execution
- **query.results**: Fetch paginated query results
- **query.cancel**: Cancel running queries

### Data Modification Zone
- **streaming.insert**: Real-time streaming inserts to tables
- **table.create**: Create new tables with schema definition
- **table.delete**: Delete tables
- **table.update**: Update table metadata and schema
- **table.patch**: Partial table updates
- **table.copy**: Copy tables within/across datasets

### Dataset Management
- **dataset.list**: List datasets in project
- **dataset.get**: Get dataset metadata
- **dataset.create**: Create new datasets
- **dataset.delete**: Delete datasets
- **dataset.update**: Update dataset properties

### Schema Management
- **schema.get**: Retrieve table schema
- **schema.update**: Modify table schema (add columns, change descriptions)
- **schema.validate**: Validate schema compatibility

### Job Management
- **job.list**: List jobs with filtering
- **job.get**: Get job status and metadata
- **job.cancel**: Cancel running jobs
- **job.query**: Submit query jobs

### Admin Zone (restricted)
- **iam.get**: Get IAM policies
- **iam.set**: Set IAM policies (requires elevated permissions)
- **routine.create**: Create stored procedures/functions
- **routine.delete**: Delete routines

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.


```
Zone 1 (Query - Read Only):
  - query.execute, query.dry_run, query.results
  - dataset.list, dataset.get
  - table.list, table.get
  - schema.get
  - job.list, job.get

Zone 2 (Data Modification):
  - streaming.insert
  - table.create, table.update, table.delete, table.copy
  - dataset.create, dataset.update
  - schema.update

Zone 3 (Admin - Restricted):
  - dataset.delete
  - iam.get, iam.set
  - routine.create, routine.delete
  - job.cancel
```

## Dependencies
- **fcp-core**: Base connector traits and types
- **fcp-oauth**: Google OAuth 2.0 authentication (service account, user credentials)
- **fcp-ratelimit**: Rate limiting for API quota management
- **fcp-telemetry**: Metrics, logging, and tracing

## Implementation Approach

### Authentication
- Service account JSON key files
- Application Default Credentials (ADC)
- User OAuth flow for interactive use
- Workload Identity for GKE deployments

### API Integration
- BigQuery REST API v2 (primary)
- Consider gcp-bigquery-client crate for Rust native bindings
- Support for both sync and async operations

### Rate Limiting
- Per-project quota management
- Concurrent query limits
- Streaming insert rate limits (rows/second, bytes/second)

### Error Handling
- Query timeout handling
- Quota exceeded errors with retry logic
- Job failure recovery
- Partial insert failure handling for streaming

## Configuration Schema

```toml
[connector.bigquery]
project_id = "my-gcp-project"
location = "US"  # or specific region

[connector.bigquery.auth]
method = "service_account"  # or "adc", "oauth"
service_account_credential_id = "cred:<service_account_json>"  # do not store service account JSON on disk

[connector.bigquery.defaults]
query_timeout_ms = 30000
max_results_per_page = 10000
use_legacy_sql = false

[connector.bigquery.streaming]
insert_batch_size = 500
insert_timeout_ms = 5000

[connector.bigquery.rate_limits]
queries_per_minute = 100
streaming_rows_per_second = 100000
```

## Testing Strategy
- Unit tests with mocked BigQuery responses
- Integration tests against BigQuery sandbox/emulator
- Cost estimation validation tests
- Schema compatibility tests

## Documentation Requirements
- Authentication setup guide (service account, ADC, OAuth)
- Query best practices (parameterization, cost control)
- Streaming insert patterns
- Error handling guide
- Rate limiting and quota management

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All query zone operations implemented and tested
- [ ] Data modification operations with proper error handling
- [ ] Admin zone with appropriate permission checks
- [ ] OAuth and service account authentication working
- [ ] Rate limiting integrated with quota management
- [ ] Comprehensive test coverage (unit + integration)
- [ ] Documentation complete with examples

Emanuel","text":"Connector already built (connectors/bigquery, 173 tests, E2E compliance passing).","created_at":"2026-03-08T23:17:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zzpa.1","title":"fcp.algolia: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zzpa.2","title":"fcp.algolia: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

Commits fa204733 + 2450534c.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:21:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zzpa.3","title":"TEST: fcp.algolia Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).
